biography.value:
Brynja Steinsdóttir is a broad-shouldered woman with calloused hands and the steady voice of someone who has been making decisions for forty people and hasn't slept in days. Before the Burning worsened, she was a trade-caravan warden — her instinct for logistics kept the refugee column alive.

She leads approximately forty people — families, children, elderly, a few traders — southwest toward Hrafnborg. She organized them into a column when the situation deteriorated and has been the practical center of their survival since.

She watches the party carefully during any exchange. She is the one who will actually decide whether the refugees trust these strangers.
biography.director:
BEAT 23 — THE ASHEN ROAD / WAYSTATION SETTLEMENT

She/her. Former trade-caravan warden. Practical, unflappable, exhausted. She has been making life-or-death decisions for forty people for two weeks and her patience for empty reassurance is zero.

ROLE: Light negotiation target. She decides whether the refugees trust the party. If the party earns her trust, the refugees may travel with them — adding 40 people to the group moving toward Hrafnborg.

DEMEANOR:

  • Interest rises: Her shoulders relax slightly. She nods — not warm, but acknowledging. "You've been honest. That's more than most."
  • Interest flat: Watching, arms folded. "Tell me something I don't know."
  • Interest drops: Her jaw tightens. She steps half a pace back from the party. "We've heard enough promises."

REFUGEE CONSEQUENCE: If refugees travel with the party, they slow movement by one day (tightens Beat 24 deadline). They are present at Hrafnborg — more mouths, more stakes, but also future seeds of recovery. In the siege (Beat 25), they add to the emotional weight of defense.

VIGMUND'S OATH NOTE: If the party swore the Advocacy Oath in Beat 13, these refugees are the living embodiment of that promise — 40 more people depending on the party's word at Hrafnborg's council table. If they also swore the Silence Oath (Beat 17), the conflict sharpens: they know about a warm refuge and cannot speak of it.

motivations: Truth about what's happening at Hrafnborg
Confirmation that the northern lights (Green Heart glow) mean what they hope
pitfalls: Urgency that tramples their need to be sure
